what_it_does: This file is a JSON array of thematic clusters (a 'carve') over project files. Each cluster groups related file paths under a tag (product, math, process), supplies a short label, a member count, an area-frequency map, a single exemplar path, and a list of member file paths.
observations: The file contains clustered groups with ids starting at 0; the visible portion runs through at least id 13 and is truncated.
Each cluster has keys: id, tag, n, label, areas, exemplar, and member_rels.
Tag values are limited to 'product', 'math', and 'process'.
The largest visible cluster is id 2 (math, n=62), drawn mostly from content-workbench/math-verification.
Process clusters are heavily concentrated in packages/elevenlabs-mcp/vault/ElevenLabs-Wizard/_discovery.
Product clusters span Visual-DNA-Vault, content-workbench/vi-workbench/surfaces/capture-recipes, and platform-integrations.
The 'areas' field is a map from top-level directory name to member count.
Several labels are visibly truncated mid-word (e.g., 'persistence classificati', '27 mcp tools, origin state map').
standouts: Cluster id 2 is the single largest math group (62 members) and is almost entirely inside content-workbench/math-verification.
Process clusters ids 5, 7, 9, and 11 are dominated by packages/elevenlabs-mcp discovery vault artifacts, suggesting an MCP/elevenlabs build audit.
Product cluster id 10 is a tightly scoped set of 34 capture-recipe files for UI surfaces.
The carve mixes design documents, code packages, and theoretical math notes under one clustering scheme.
conventions: Top-level JSON array of cluster objects.
Tag taxonomy limited to product, math, process.
Areas represented as a frequency map keyed by top-level directory.
Exemplar is a single member path chosen to represent the cluster.
File paths use forward slashes and include READMEs, MOCs, round notes, and vault metadata.
Truncated labels suggest a fixed display width or length limit.
concerns: File is truncated, so the full cluster set is unknown.
Labels are frequently cut off, reducing interpretability.
No explicit overlap or conflict metadata for files that appear in multiple clusters.
No provenance, timestamp, or clustering algorithm metadata.
No score or confidence indicating why each exemplar was selected.
notes: This is a thematic index of project files produced by a migration wizard run, useful as a map but incomplete due to truncation and lacking clustering provenance.
questions: What clustering algorithm or criteria produced these groups?
Why was each exemplar chosen over other members?
How should overlapping cluster membership be resolved?
What do the truncated labels and file continuation contain?
Is this carve a snapshot, and if so, when was it generated?
purpose_vs_actual: The file appears to be a thematic carve/ledger of code and design substrates, which matches its content; it does not include provenance or algorithmic details that would fully explain how the carve was produced.
apparent_themes: Thematic clustering of heterogeneous project artifacts
Math verification as a distinct knowledge domain
Product surface specification via capture recipes and platform blueprints
intention_signals: It seems to be trying to build a navigable, human-interpretable map of a large, multi-vault project so that migration or integration work can proceed from representative exemplars rather than raw file trees.
novelty: It applies an interpretive layer—tags, labels, exemplars, and area distributions—to a file tree, treating code and design documents as a single substrate ledger rather than separating source from documentation.
contribution: It contributes a thematic index and exemplar-based entry points into a sprawling set of vaults and packages, which a fused system could use for orientation, search seeding, and cluster-aware navigation.
summary_for_embedding: A JSON ledger that clusters project files into interpretive groups labeled product, math, or process, recording each group's size, area distribution, exemplar path, and member file list.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a thematic, exemplar-driven map of code and design substrates for a migration wizard run.
